markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Prove core CRDT behavior does not require an external sync server or manually launched relay. This MT belongs to CRDTAndConcurrencyCore. Make rich document, graph mutation, and agent proposal state conflict-aware and recoverable.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
CRDT requirements apply to human plus agent concurrent draft state, rich document block state, graph mutation proposals, and EventLedger promotion, even when realtime multi-user UI is deferred.
AI/agent actions must carry stable actor/site IDs, leases, idempotency keys, and recovery receipts.
scope.expected_behavior: CrdtNoExternalRelayProof leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CrdtNoExternalRelayProof is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 ea0a601b; CRDTAndConcurrencyCore: 41 tests/6 binaries green on real PostgreSQL. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: STATIC SCAN is real and reasonably complete. static_scan_finds_no_external_relay_dependency (knowledge_crdt_proof_tests.rs:380-436) asserts Cargo.toml declares none of {y-websocket, hocuspocus, yrs-warp, y-sync, liveblocks, partykit, sharedb, automerge-repo-network}, AND scans every .rs in src/kernel/crdt plus api/knowledge_crdt.rs for forbidden tokens {ws://, wss://, y-websocket, hocuspocus, liveblocks, partykit, sync-server, tokio_tungstenite}, asserting scanned.len() > 10. I independently grepped the same surfaces plus src/storage/knowledge_crdt.rs: the only 'sqlite' hits are in PROHIBITION contexts (offline boundary denied list + a 'NO SQLite' comment); no relay tokens anywhere. Confirmed.

RUNTIME PROOF is real. full_draft_cycle_needs_only_postgres (line 442-497) runs a two-actor draft cycle entirely against local PostgreSQL and asserts every update_bytes_ref starts with 'postgres://' and the event stream id is 'knowledge-crdt:<doc>'. No relay process is started or contacted.

COVERAGE GAP (note): the static scan does NOT include src/storage/knowledge_crdt.rs (the storage layer) -- only the crdt module dir + the one API file. Storage is pure sqlx so the risk is low, but a relay client added there would slip past this specific test. Also the scan greps source TEXT, so it is defeatable by string obfuscation; for an honest no-relay posture it is adequate, not adversarial-proof.

NOT TAUTOLOGICAL: this is a filesystem+dependency scan plus a real DB round-trip, not an assertion that a constant equals itself. Distinct from the weaker offline-boundary contract self-check in MT-073/MT-080.
evasion_probes: Independent grep -E 'y-websocket|hocuspocus|liveblocks|partykit|tokio_tungstenite|ws://|wss://|sqlite|rusqlite' over crdt+storage+api: only prohibition-context hits. No external relay.

Checked whether durable bytes could leave Postgres: update_bytes_ref is always 'postgres://kernel_crdt_updates/...' (yjs_bridge.rs:313) and snapshot refs 'postgres://kernel_crdt_snapshots/...' -- enforced by validate_crdt_update_record (proven in MT-080's filesystem-authority rejection).
Checked the scan breadth assertion (scanned.len() > 10): the crdt dir has 22 .rs files, so the guard is satisfied and meaningful.
evidence_checked: independent grep over src/kernel/crdt/, src/storage/knowledge_crdt.rs, src/api/knowledge_crdt.rs
Rerun status: test rerun deferred (tree mid-foreign-WIP, knowledge_code_index wired into lib.rs:180); static review stands.

evidence: Adversarial code review PASS/PASS_WITH_NOTES (ADVERSARIAL_CODE_REVIEWER-20260611-PM); core sound, only defense-in-depth notes.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
